senses.definition: in a way that is effortless or easy

examples.x: She won the chess tournament hands down, outclassing her opponents in every match.
With his experience, he completed the complex project hands down, finishing well ahead of schedule.
The skilled musician could play the difficult piece hands down, effortlessly showcasing his talent.
The team's dedication and hard work ensured they won the championship hands down, with a significant lead in the competition.
Her natural athletic ability made her the fastest sprinter on the track, and she won the race hands down.

word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪk ɐ hˈɑːt nˈaɪf θɹuː bˈʌɾɚ

examples.x: An industrial machine like that can cut through any material like a knife through butter.
The bill passed through the senate like a hot knife through butter.
With his sharp negotiating skills, he cut through the complex contract like a knife through butter.
Her powerful performance on stage moved the audience, and her voice cut through the silence like a knife through butter.

senses.definition: to need to put a lot of hard work or energy into doing something

examples.x: That test was so easy, I aced it without even breaking a sweat.
I had to break a sweat to finish that marathon; it was a grueling race.
She's so talented at solving puzzles; she can complete a difficult crossword without breaking a sweat.
The construction workers had to break a sweat to complete the project ahead of schedule.
With determination and hard work, he passed the physical fitness test without breaking a sweat.

senses.definition: to progress without facing problems or difficulties

examples.x: They anticipated difficulties in the negotiation, but it turned out to be clear sailing with no major disputes.

senses.definition: (of a learned skill) still easy to do after not practicing for long

examples.x: You see, learning to swim is like riding a bicycle.
I haven't played the piano in years, but it's just like riding a bike; I still remember how to play.
Cooking that family recipe is like riding a bicycle; I can do it without looking at the instructions.
After a long break from coding, I found it's like riding a bike; the programming knowledge came back to me quickly.

word_list.word: [take] to {sth} like a duck to water
word_list.pronunciation: tˈeɪk tʊ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ lˈaɪk ɐ dˈʌk tə wˈɔːɾɚ

senses.definition: to be really good at something as soon as one starts to do it

examples.x: Sarah took to her bike like a duck to water.
He moved to a foreign country and took to the language like a duck to water; he's now fluent in no time.

senses.definition: said to mean that something can be easily done or achieved
used to refer to a really easy task or undertaking

examples.x: Compared to my previous job, this one is a walk in the park; it's much less stressful.
For her, solving complex math problems is like a walk in the park; she excels in mathematics.
Cooking a simple pasta dish is a walk in the park; it only takes a few minutes.
Completing the project was a walk in the park with the help of the efficient team.
He's a natural at public speaking, so giving that speech was a walk in the park for him.

senses.definition: the easiest achievable task in a given situation

examples.x: We should start with the low-hanging fruit, the simple fixes that will immediately improve our process.
In the project, the easy tasks are the low-hanging fruit, while the more complex ones will take time.
When setting goals, it's a good idea to begin with the low-hanging fruit and then tackle the more challenging ones.
To improve efficiency, the company first addressed the low-hanging fruit, such as reducing unnecessary paperwork.
In terms of improving your health, cutting out sugary snacks is a piece of low-hanging fruit.

senses.definition: said to mean that something is very easy to find out

examples.x: No prizes for guessing who forgot their keys again; it's always John.
It's no secret; no prizes for guessing that the restaurant is crowded during lunch hours.
No prizes for guessing that she's going to choose the chocolate dessert; it's her favorite.
With all the traffic and noise, there are no prizes for guessing why he prefers the quiet countryside.
No prizes for guessing that the team with the most experienced players won the championship.

senses.definition: the fastest or easiest way in which something can be done or dealt with

examples.x: Human nature is generally of such kind that it will choose a line of least resistance if that is at all possible, and loathe exertion beyond it.
They would rather take the line of least resistance than become involved in arguments.
Instead of trying to convince him to change his mind, she chose the path of least resistance and agreed with his plan.
In problem-solving, finding the path of least resistance often leads to the quickest and most effective solutions.
To achieve success in the project, the team focused on the line of least resistance, avoiding unnecessary obstacles.

senses.definition: to experience no or very few difficulties or disruptions, particularly after a period of hardship

examples.x: We'll be in smooth waters if we can secure a new loan extension from the bank.
Ever since they implemented the new procedures, the project has been in smooth water.
His career has been in smooth water for the past few years, with no major setbacks.
The business was in smooth water until the recent economic downturn.

senses.definition: to be achievable or available for someone without much difficulty or time

word_list.pronunciation: fˈɔːl ˌɪntʊ ɔːɹ dɹˈɑːp ˌɪntʊ ɔːɹ lˈænd ˌɪntʊ wˈʌnz lˈæp

senses.definition: to receive something unexpectedly and without effort
a benefit that is obtained at the cost or with the effort of another person

examples.x: A group of students who have been assigned to do a report where only one student cares, does all the work and the others go along for a free ride.
Only those who share the work can share the benefits - nobody gets a free ride!
Sarah always gets a free ride from her parents, living at home without paying rent or bills.
He's been getting a free ride in the group project, not doing any work but still getting credit.
It's not fair that he's getting a free ride on his taxes, while the rest of us are paying our fair share.

word_list.pronunciation: wɪð wˈʌn hˈænd ɔːɹ ˈɑːɹm tˈaɪd ɔːɹ bɪhˌaɪnd wˈʌnz bˈæk

senses.definition: in an easy manner and without facing many difficulties

examples.x: The chess grandmaster played against multiple opponents simultaneously, effortlessly winning with one hand behind his back.
The skilled musician flawlessly played a complex composition on the piano, showcasing his extraordinary talent as if doing it with one hand behind his back.
The expert juggler amazed the audience by effortlessly juggling multiple objects, skillfully demonstrating his mastery with one hand behind his back.
Her part wasn't very demanding - she could have played it with one arm tied behind her back.
The club is having to improve the team with one hand tied behind its back because it has no money to spend on new players.
